title: cuphea procumbens
intro: Cuphea procumbens, also known as firecracker plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Cuphea procumbens include its potential to soothe sore throats and reduce congestion, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Cuphea procumbens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing users to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
